div.head:
No. 237
Editorial Note
div.p: By the end of 1953 agreement had been reached among the United States, the United Kingdom, France, and the Soviet Union to hold a four-power conference in Berlin to discuss, among other things, the future of Germany. The conference opened January 25 in the Allied Control Authority building in West Berlin and met until February 18 when it adjourned without results. For documentation on the conference, see Documents 257 ff.
